rationale: The actual resolution was a terse duplicate-closure pointing to #4808/#4809, and X is the only candidate that explicitly reaches and recommends that exact conclusion ('closing as duplicate is the right call... keep #4891 closed as a duplicate'), while also flagging the substantive risk that #4808 never merged and the regression test doesn't cover this file. Y produces a competent technical root-cause/fix/verification writeup but never frames the issue as a duplicate closure, instead treating it as an open bug to patch — a meaningful mismatch with how the thread was actually resolved. Y's output also duplicates its entire draft_answer section verbatim, suggesting a pipeline glitch that dents reliability. Both are similarly grounded in plausible file/line citations, but X is more transparent about the boundary of what it could verify (rebase branch vs. upstream main).
